commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: "God will hear me, and He shall humble them That is before ages" (ver. For they rely on some leader or other of theirs that hath begun but yesterday. "He shall humble them That is before ages. " For even if with reference to time Christ is of Mary the Virgin, nevertheless before ages: "In the beginning He is the Word and the Word with God, and the Word God. " "He shall humble them That is before ages. For to them is no changing:" of them I "speak to whom is no changing. " He knew of some to persevere, and in the perseverance of their own wickedness to die. For we see them, and to them is no changing: they that die in that same perverseness, in that same schism, to them is no changing. God shall humble them, shall humble them in damnation, because they are exalted in dissension. To them is no changing, because they are not changed for the better, but for the worse: neither while they are here, nor in the resurrection. For all we shall rise again, but not all shall be changed. Because "'To them is no changing: and they have not feared God.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Change, or redemption for them, (Psalm xliii. 13.; Eusebius) or they will not amend, (Worthington) nor cease to blaspheme God, and to calumniate me. (Calmet)
Eternal. Literally, "He who is before ages. "(Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
